landing_signin_required_toast: Please sign in to access this page
landing_hero_tagline: Together, We Map - Open. Accurate. Accessible
landing_about_description: DroneTM is an integrated digital public good solution that aims to harness the power of the crowd to help generate high-resolution aerial maps of any location. Its innovative platform allows drone pilots in developing countries to access job opportunities and contribute to creating high-resolution datasets for disaster response and community resilience.
landing_footer_copyright: © Drone Arial Tasking Manager. All Rights Reserved 2024
landing_mobile_app_description: DroneTM is an integrated open source digital public good solution that aims to harness the power of the crowd to help generate high-resolution aerial maps to improve resilience of the disaster prone communities across the world.
landing_open_source_description: An open-source drone tasking platform to coordinate aerial survey activities for target project areas among multiple pilots, incorporating tools and processes to ensure coordinated flight plans for imagery acquisition, and a seamless pipeline for processing and dissemination.
landing_talk_to_us_other_queries: For other queries mail us at
landing_accordion_crisis_response_desc: The Drone Tasking Manager will enable faster, more precise deployment of drones during emergencies, improving resource allocation and decision-making in critical situations. This will lead to quicker and more effective crisis interventions.
landing_accordion_community_empowerment_: The Drone Tasking Manager will empower local communities by providing them with access to real-time aerial data and insights. This tool will enhance their ability to participate in decision-making processes, improve disaster preparedness, and support local initiatives with accurate, timely information.
landing_accordion_cost_reduction_desc: The Drone Tasking Manager will lower operational costs by optimizing drone tasking, reducing the need for manual interventions, and minimizing resource wastage. This efficiency will lead to significant savings in both time and money during operations.
landing_accordion_data_accessibility_des: The Drone Tasking Manager will provide users with easy access to drone data, enabling better decision-making and more informed actions. This centralized data access will ensure that critical information is readily available to all stakeholders.
landing_roles_project_managers_item_2: Plan a flight for the desired imagery characteristics such as flying height, overlaps, angles, Ground Sampling Distance (GSD, essentially resolution), etc.
landing_roles_drone_operators_item_1: Register to participate in imagery acquisition
landing_roles_drone_operators_item_2: Select areas that they are interested in flying from the overall project area
landing_roles_drone_operators_item_3: Download flight plans appropriate to their hardware and capacity
landing_roles_drone_operators_item_4: Operate the drone by pushing a “Fly mission” button, causing the drone to cover the assigned area automatically
landing_roles_drone_operators_item_5: Upload the resulting images to a processing server
landing_roles_data_users_item_2: Define desired imagery characteristics such as Ground Sampling Distance (GSD, essentially resolution), overlap, angles, etc.
landing_rationale_limited_access_title: Limited Access to High Resolution Aerial Datasets
landing_rationale_limited_access_desc: In low-income and disaster-prone areas, access to near real-time satellite datasets is severely limited. High-resolution satellite imagery, when available, is often expensive and out-of-date. Additionally, full-scale aircraft mapping is not a viable option due to high costs and operational complexity.
landing_rationale_limited_engagement_des: Traditional mapping solutions involve professional consultants with expensive equipment who engage with communities only briefly, which can lead to delays and lack of locally relevant data.
landing_rationale_proprietary_desc: Many existing drone operation tools are proprietary and not designed for large-scale collaborative efforts, which limits their usefulness for community-driven projects
landing_rationale_compliance_desc: The use of drones, especially in sensitive or conflict-prone areas, raises significant safety and regulatory concerns.
landing_features_user_management_desc: Simplifies the onboarding process, enhances security with role-based access, and facilitates efficient management of participant activities.
landing_features_survey_project_desc: Streamlines project setup, allows for custom project specifications, and ensures all participants clear guidelines and objectives.
landing_features_flight_plan_desc: Ensures consistency and accuracy in flight plans, maximizes area coverage, and minimizes data gaps.
landing_features_photogrammetric_desc: Provides a seamless workflow from data capture to processing, quickly turning raw images into actionable insights.
landing_features_notifications_desc: Keeps all users informed and engaged, ensures timely responses to project needs, and enhances collaborative efforts.
landing_features_data_viz_desc: Aids in better understanding and interpretation of the data, supports decision-making processes, and enhances presentation of findings.
landing_about_motivation_desc: Drones belonging to and operated by communities are more effective because they are in place and can respond immediately, responsibly, and take into account the needs of the community as they see them.
landing_about_vision_desc: A user-friendly, inclusive application suite enabling anyone with access to a drone, even an inexpensive consumer or DIY drone, to contribute easily and effectively to a global free and open aerial imagery repository.
landing_case_freetown_title: Community-Led Drone Mapping in Freetown City
landing_case_freetown_desc: 30 Freetown residents used DroneTM and low-cost drones to map 80km² of the city at 5cm resolution. The imagery now drives urban planning, flood preparedness, infrastructure management, and accessibility assessments across the city.
landing_case_dominica_title: Drone Imagery for Climate Resilience in Dominica
landing_case_dominica_desc: After repeated hurricane disasters, HOT drone imagery of Coulibistrie supported a community resilience workshop where 30 residents mapped flood risks and shaped local response planning - demonstrating how open aerial data empowers communities to prepare for climate impacts.
landing_case_mexico_location: La Paz, Baja California Sur, Mexico
landing_case_mexico_title: Mangrove Monitoring with Low-Cost Drones in Mexico
landing_case_mexico_desc: Local community groups used DroneTM to capture high-resolution imagery of threatened mangroves, generating 3D models and openly published orthophotos. This enables ongoing monitoring of mangrove cover, vegetation health, and coastal change at a fraction of traditional costs.
auth_forgot_password_instructions: Enter the email address and we will send you a link to reset your password.
auth_reset_password_error: Error resetting password. Please try again.
auth_hanko_failed: Failed to authenticate. Please try logging in again.
profile_password_min_length: Password must have at least 8 characters

create_basic_info_description: Fill in your basic project information such as name and description
create_basic_name_required: Name of the project is required
create_basic_name_exists: A project with this name already exists
create_aoi_upload_or_draw_save_project: Please upload or draw and save project area
create_aoi_upload_or_draw_save_nfz: Please upload or draw and save No Fly zone area
create_params_altitude_placeholder: Enter Altitude From Ground in meter
create_params_country_max_altitude: Since your project is in {country}, the maximum allowable drone altitude is {altitude} m. Please proceed further if you have permission to fly higher.
create_params_overlap_guidance_terrain: Terrain and elevation models need 85% or higher on both axes for accurate triangulation. Side overlap below 70% is not recommended.
create_params_overlap_guidance_orthophot: For orthophotos, 75% front and 65% side is a reliable minimum.
create_params_overlap_guidance_low_textu: Low-texture areas (sand, water, bare ground, dense vegetation) and mountainous terrain benefit from higher overlaps to ensure sufficient matching points between images.
create_params_overlap_guidance_floor: Never go below 72% front overlap as a practical floor.
create_params_advanced_distance_placehol: Enter Distance Between Paths in meter
create_params_desc_line_orientation: How many perpendicular passes should the drone make ?
create_params_desc_straighten: Straighten the leg of the flight, so that it removes the curves in the flight.
create_params_desc_all_points: Generate every point as a waypoint. Sometimes crashes if you open the map in the DJI FLy App.
create_params_desc_image_overlap: Overlap between images in the flight plan. Will modify the value of “ Distance Between Paths” and “speed” based on altitude, gimbal angle, overlap, and photo interval.
create_params_info_gsd_desc: GSD in a digital photo of the ground from air is the distance between pixel centers measured on the ground.
create_params_info_altitude_desc: The altitude at which the drone should fly during the mission, in meters.
create_params_info_front_overlap_desc: The percentage of overlap between consecutive images taken in the forward direction.
create_params_info_side_overlap_desc: The percentage of overlap between images captured on adjacent flight lines.
create_params_info_orthophoto_desc: 2D orthophoto is a geometrically corrected aerial image that can be used as a map with consistent scale and accurate measurements.
create_params_info_dtm_desc: DTM represents the bare earth surface, excluding objects and showing only the terrain's elevation.
create_params_info_dsm_desc: DSM is a 3D representation of the Earth's surface including all features like buildings and vegetation.
create_params_info_dem_desc: The Digital Elevation Model (DEM) file that will be used to generate the terrain follow flight plan. This file should be in GeoTIFF format.
create_generate_description: Split the task into smaller chunks based on the given dimensions to ensure more efficient and precise data collection and analysis.
create_generate_guidelines_title: When setting up your task area, please consider the following recommendations based on the equipment and parameters used by the drone operators:
create_generate_guideline_rc2_terrain: If the drone operators are using the RC2 controller with the terrain following parameter, it's recommended to create a square with dimensions up to 300 meters, covering approximately 0.1 km².
create_generate_guideline_rcn2_terrain: If the drone operators are using the RC-N2 controller with the terrain following parameter or the RC2 controller without the terrain following parameter, create a square with dimensions up to 550 meters, covering approximately 0.3 km².
create_generate_guideline_rcn2_no_terrai: If the drone operators are using the RC-N2 controller without the terrain following parameter, the recommended square dimensions are up to 1000 meters, covering approximately 1 km².
create_generate_guidelines_conclusion: These guidelines will help ensure that your task area is appropriately sized based on the drone equipment and parameters being used. This will also reduce the chances of RC lagging while flying the drone.
create_generate_waypoints_avg: The average number of waypoints is:
create_contributions_instructions_desc: Detailed instructions or parameters for the drone operation.
create_contributions_deadline_desc: Date for specifying when the project should be submitted.
create_contributions_regulator_key: Does this project require approval from the local regulatory committee?
create_contributions_regulator_desc: Indicate if the project requires approval from the local regulatory committee before proceeding.
create_contributions_regulator_email_des: The email addresses of local regulatory committee members. If one of them approves or rejects the project, it will be processed accordingly. This is required for areas where drone flights are restricted and require permission.
create_contributions_lock_approval_desc: Approval required tasks should be approved from project creator to proceed with flying.
create_contributions_regulator_topic: Does this project require approval from the local regulatory committee ?
create_project_setup_incomplete: Please complete the project setup steps before creating the project.
create_project_enter_gsd: Please enter the project GSD before creating the project.
create_project_enter_altitude: Please enter the flight altitude before creating the project.

import_description: Point at a public S3-compatible bucket. We'll scan EXIF GPS from each JPEG (subdirectories up to 3 levels deep), build a buffered AOI, and create a drone-tm project. Imagery transfer and ingestion are run separately afterwards.
import_required_fields_error: Endpoint, bucket name, and project name are required
import_submitted_message: Please be patient while the project is created in the background. For many images, this may take several hours. The new project will appear on your projects list once it has been created.
image_review_no_classified_images: No classified images available for review.
image_review_select_multiple_help: Drag a rectangle to select multiple images
image_review_click_task_area_assign: Click a task area to assign
image_review_review_grouped_images: Review the classified images grouped by tasks.
image_review_double_click_thumbnail_help: Double-click a thumbnail to inspect the full image and handle issues.
image_review_duplicate_description: This image is a duplicate of an existing image
image_review_classification_override_not: This will override the automatic classification result.
image_review_classification_override_eac: This will override the automatic classification result for each image.
image_review_cleanup_confirm_body: This will delete the imagery marked as invalid or unmatched during the uploading process. It is not reversible. Are you sure?
flight_gap_review_finalize_desc: Review and finalize gaps before processing a reconstructed flight plan.
flight_gap_drone_model_help: Select the aircraft to use for reflight plan generation.
flight_gap_review_detected_gap_desc: Review the detected gap area, choose a drone model, then generate a reflight plan.
flight_gap_select_drone_model_first: Please select a drone model first.
flight_gap_gaps_updated_no_plan: Gaps updated. No flight plan generated.
imagery_upload_ingestion_started_success: Ingestion job started. Images will appear in the Classify dialog as they are processed.
imagery_upload_keep_success: Images uploaded. Open Classify Imagery to process them.
imagery_upload_success_continue: Upload successful. Click Continue to finalise.
imagery_upload_existing_s3_prompt: Already uploaded imagery directly to S3 that you need to ingest?
imagery_upload_keep_images_body: You have uploaded images in this session. Would you like to keep them for classification later, or delete them?
imagery_upload_are_you_sure: Are you sure? This cannot be undone.
classify_imagery_complete_toast: Classification complete! Use the Verify dialog to review results.
classify_imagery_no_stuck_images: No stuck images found. Try starting classification again.
classify_imagery_reset_stuck_success: Reset {count} stuck image(s). You can now re-classify.
classify_imagery_scan_already_running: Scan is already running. New images will appear here once they are ingested.
classify_imagery_scan_started: Scan started. Any untracked user-upload images will be added for classification.
classify_imagery_pending_help: This will classify all pending images in the project across all upload sessions.
classify_imagery_reset_stuck_title: Reset images stuck in classification for more than 10 minutes
classify_imagery_issue_rate_warning: {percentage}% of your images ({issues} out of {total}) have issues.
classify_imagery_complete_help: Close this dialog and open Verify Imagery to review results and inspect any issues.
classify_imagery_upload_first: Upload images first, then return here to classify them.
classify_imagery_disable_flight_tail_det: Skip automatic rejection of takeoff/landing transit imagery. Use this if detection is producing false positives on your dataset.
drone_task_missing_dem_blocked: This task has terrain-follow enabled. The DEM may still be downloading and processing in the background - this typically takes a few minutes after project creation. For safety, mission generation is blocked until the DEM is ready.
drone_task_missing_dem_override: If you understand the risk, you can still generate the mission without a DEM.
drone_task_missing_dem_canceled: Mission generation canceled - DEM not ready yet.
drone_task_flight_time_note: *This flight time was calculated using an average ground speed of 11.5 m/s.
drone_task_download_error: There was an error while downloading file {error}
drone_task_send_file_error: There was an error while sending file: {error}
drone_task_mark_fully_flown_error: Failed to mark task as fully flown
drone_task_manual_override_description: Last-resort failsafe to recover a stuck task. Bypasses the normal workflow and may leave downstream processing in an inconsistent state - only use if no other action works.
drone_task_manual_override_confirm_body: This will force the task into {state}, bypassing the normal state-machine transitions. The action is auditable but cannot be undone - it may also leave downstream workflows (imagery, processing) in an inconsistent state.
drone_task_manual_override_last_resort: Use only as a last resort.

individual_project_revert_task_confirm: Are you sure you want to revert this task to its previous state?

processing_dialog_task_processing_desc: Processes images for each task individually to produce a quick preview orthophoto.

processing_dialog_scaleodm_prompt:
Enter a ScaleODM server URL for processing
(e.g. https://scaleodm.example.com/)

processing_dialog_no_tasks_ready_title: No tasks are ready for processing yet.
processing_dialog_no_tasks_ready_help: Upload imagery and mark tasks ready for processing in the Verify Imagery step first.
processing_dialog_final_processing_desc: Stitches all task images together into a single high-quality orthophoto and 3D model. This takes longer.
processing_dialog_gcp_saved: GCP file saved for this project.
processing_dialog_gcp_saved_help: The saved gcp.txt will be included in final processing.
processing_dialog_gcp_not_yet_help: Mark points on images with the GCP Editor, or upload an existing gcp.txt.
processing_dialog_coverage_below_warning: Coverage is below 90%. You can still process, but the results may be affected.
processing_dialog_already_in_progress: Final processing is already in progress.
processing_dialog_no_tasks_available: No project tasks are available yet.
processing_dialog_no_imagery_available: No tasks have imagery available for processing. Upload imagery first.
processing_dialog_final_in_progress: Final processing is in progress. Check the queue for details.
processing_dialog_final_started_success: Final processing started - this may take a long time.
processing_dialog_start_failed_count: Failed to start processing for {count} task(s)
processing_dialog_final_results_desc: Download the outputs from the completed final processing run.
processing_dialog_queue_desc: Monitor active processing and refresh to see queue movement. Note: newly submitted tasks may take a few minutes to appear here while images are being prepared and uploaded.
processing_dialog_no_queue_tasks: No tasks on the processing server.
processing_dialog_no_queue_tasks_desc: Refresh this panel to check for queue changes.
processing_dialog_queue_position: Your project is approximately #{position} in the queue.
processing_dialog_queue_position_empty: Queue position will appear here when this project has an active ODM job.
processing_dialog_final_confirm_intro: This will process {processable} of {total} tasks into a single orthophoto and 3D model. This may take a long time.
processing_dialog_final_confirm_unverifi: NOTE: {count} task(s) have imagery but were not individually verified/marked as ready. They will be included in processing.
processing_dialog_final_confirm_coverage:
WARNING: Only {percentage}% of the project area has imagery coverage.

With incomplete coverage:
- Gaps may appear in the final orthophoto where no imagery exists
- Alignment may suffer near uncovered areas, causing slight distortion
- Edge areas near gaps may have reduced accuracy due to fewer tie points
processing_dialog_final_confirm_ondemand: ON-DEMAND MODE: Processing will run on a dedicated on-demand node and will not be interrupted.
processing_dialog_final_confirm_proceed: Are you sure you want to proceed?
map_popup_regulator_pending: Unable to proceed, local regulator's approval is pending.
map_popup_regulator_rejected: Unable to proceed, local regulators rejected the project

map_popup_desc_has_issues: This task has been flagged as not flyable.
map_popup_desc_ready_for_processing: Images verified and ready for processing{byLocker}.
